ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2, Daily

ElementDescription: In General: Please describe your overall approach to the task in your own words, as best you can.
In General: Did button-tapping in the task feel similar to any behaviors you do in the real world? If so, what behaviors did it feel similar to, and how was it similar?

In General: Did you experience obsessions/compulsions during the task? Examples of obsessions/compulsions people might experience during the task include: - wanting to tap a special number of times, or an odd or even number of times. - wanting to use each finger a certain amount, or "even out" button-pressing across fingers, keys, or parts of the screen. - avoiding or preferring certain ratings on the sliders. - Obsessions/compulsions similar to what you typically experience outside the task.

In General: Please describe the content of the obsessions/compulsions in as much detail as you can. What were the obsessions / compulsions? When did they occur? How did they affect your behavior in the task?
In General: How distressing were the obsessions/compulsions you experienced during the task?
In General: How intense were the obsessions/compulsions you experienced during the task?
In General: How much did the obsessions/compulsions you experienced during the task affect your behavior during the task: your ratings or the amount of tapping?
